title: quercus lusitanica var. subocculata
intro: Quercus lusitanica var. subocculata, also known as the Portuguese oak, is a plant species that belongs to the beech family and is native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues. The medicinal properties of Quercus lusitanica var. subocculata are attributed to its high content of tannins and flavonoids, which have been shown to have antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties. Quercus lusitanica var. subocculata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and other respiratory issues, as well as to support digestive health.
